name: Chef Elias Müller
bio: A Berlin native who studied philosophy before turning to food, Elias cooks to ask questions, not answer them. He plays with textures of warmth: melted cheese, smoked potatoes, broths that linger. He peppers his dinners with small riddles for guests to solve between courses.
eventDescription: Diners move through a progression of comforting dishes made strange: truffled dumplings under hay smoke, bitter-sweet stews, and a dessert of chocolate ashes and milk snow. The evening ends with Elias sharing a question, not a speech: “What is comfort to you?”
